Title: Successful User Registration with Valid Credentials
Preconditions: User is on the registration page of the application.
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., testuser@example.com), valid password (e.g., P@ssw0rd123), valid name (e.g., John Doe), valid age (e.g., 25)
Test Steps:
1. User enters a valid email address in the email field.
2. User enters a valid password in the password field.
3. User enters a valid name in the name field.
4. User enters a valid age (18 or older) in the age field.
5. User clicks the "Register" button.
Expected Result: User account is successfully created, and a confirmation message is displayed. User is redirected to the login page.

Title: Successful User Login with Valid Credentials
Preconditions: User has a registered account with valid credentials.
Test Data: Registered email (e.g., testuser@example.com), correct password (e.g., P@ssw0rd123)
Test Steps:
1. User enters the registered email address in the email field.
2. User enters the correct password in the password field.
3. User clicks the "Login" button.
Expected Result: User is successfully logged in, and the application redirects the user to the home page or dashboard.

Title: Successful Bug Reporting by Logged-in User
Preconditions: User is logged in to the application.
Test Data: Valid bug description (e.g., "App crashes when opening profile"), valid category (e.g., "Technical Issue"), screenshot (optional)
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Report Bug" section.
2. User selects a valid category for the bug.
3. User enters a detailed description of the bug in the provided text field.
4. User attaches a screenshot (if applicable).
5. User clicks the "Submit" button.
Expected Result: Bug report is successfully submitted, and a confirmation message is displayed. The bug report appears in the system’s bug tracking section.

Title: Successful User Reporting for Violations
Preconditions: User is logged in and has encountered a violation by another user.
Test Data: Valid user ID (e.g., 12345), valid reason for reporting (e.g., "Spam or inappropriate content"), additional details (optional)
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the profile of the user to be reported.
2. User clicks the "Report" button.
3. User selects a valid reason for reporting (e.g., "Spam or inappropriate content").
4. User enters additional details (if applicable).
5. User clicks the "Submit Report" button.
Expected Result: Report is successfully submitted, and a confirmation message is displayed. The reported user’s profile is flagged for review by administrators.

Title: User Receives Notification for New Message
Preconditions: User A is logged in, and User B sends a direct message to User A.
Test Data: Valid message content (e.g., "Hello, how are you?"), recipient (User A)
Test Steps:
1. User B sends a direct message to User A.
2. User A logs out and logs back in.
Expected Result: User A receives a notification indicating a new direct message. The notification includes the sender’s name and a brief preview of the message.

Title: Sending and Receiving Direct Messages Between Users
Preconditions: User A and User B are both logged in.
Test Data: Valid message content (e.g., "Let’s discuss the product"), recipient (User B)
Test Steps:
1. User A navigates to the "Messages" section.
2. User A selects User B from the contact list.
3. User A enters a message in the text field.
4. User A clicks the "Send" button.
5. User B logs in and checks the "Messages" section.
Expected Result: User B receives the message from User A in their inbox. The message appears in the conversation history with User A.

Title: Viewing Another User’s Public Profile
Preconditions: User A is logged in, and User B has a public profile.
Test Steps:
1. User A searches for User B by name or ID.
2. User A clicks on User B’s profile link.
Expected Result: User A’s screen displays User B’s public profile, including name, age, and any publicly shared posts or items.

Title: Creating a New Post to Sell/Buy an Item
Preconditions: User is logged in and has an item to list.
Test Data: Valid item name (e.g., "Used Laptop"), valid description (e.g., "Good condition, 500€"), valid category (e.g., "Electronics"), valid price (e.g., 500), valid images (optional)
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Create Post" section.
2. User enters the item name in the title field.
3. User selects a valid category for the item.
4. User enters a detailed description of the item.
5. User enters the price of the item.
6. User uploads images (if applicable).
7. User clicks the "Post" button.
Expected Result: The post is successfully created and displayed on the user’s profile and in the relevant category feed. A confirmation message is shown to the user.

Title: Successful User Registration with Valid Data
Preconditions: User has the app installed and opens it for the first time
Test Data: Valid name, surname, date of birth (majority), valid email, strong password
Test Steps:
1. User opens the app for the first time.
2. User is prompted to register.
3. User enters valid name, surname, date of birth (majority), email, and password.
4. User confirms registration.
Expected Result: User account is created successfully, and the user is redirected to the login screen.

Title: User Registration with Invalid Date of Birth (Minor)
Preconditions: User has the app installed and opens it for the first time
Test Data: Valid name, surname, date of birth (minor), valid email, strong password
Test Steps:
1. User opens the app for the first time.
2. User is prompted to register.
3. User enters valid name, surname, date of birth (minor), email, and password.
4. User confirms registration.
Expected Result: Registration fails with an error message indicating the user must be of legal age.

Title: User Registration with Existing Email
Preconditions: User has the app installed and opens it for the first time; another user is already registered with the same email
Test Data: Valid name, surname, date of birth (majority), existing email, strong password
Test Steps:
1. User opens the app for the first time.
2. User is prompted to register.
3. User enters valid name, surname, date of birth (majority), existing email, and password.
4. User confirms registration.
Expected Result: Registration fails with an error message indicating the email is already in use.

Title: User Login with Invalid Credentials
Preconditions: User is registered and has the app installed
Test Steps:
1. User opens the app.
2. User selects the login option.
3. User enters valid email and incorrect password.
4. User confirms login.
Expected Result: Login fails with an error message indicating invalid credentials.

Title: User Login Without Registration
Preconditions: User has the app installed but is not registered
Test Steps:
1. User opens the app.
2. User selects the login option.
3. User attempts to enter credentials.
Expected Result: User is prompted to register instead of logging in.

Title: Bug Report via Profile Settings
Preconditions: User is logged in and has accessed the profile settings
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the app.
2. User navigates to profile settings.
3. User locates and clicks the bug report button.
4. User fills out the bug report form with a description.
5. User submits the bug report.
Expected Result: Bug report is submitted successfully, and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Bug Report via GitHub (No Profile Access)
Preconditions: User is logged in but cannot access profile settings
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the app.
2. User identifies the GitHub issue button (Target logo).
3. User clicks the GitHub issue button.
4. User fills out the issue form with a description.
5. User submits the issue.
Expected Result: GitHub issue is created successfully, and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Delivery Personnel Reports User for Misconduct
Preconditions: Delivery personnel is logged in and has accessed a user profile for a misbehaving user
Test Steps:
1. Delivery personnel logs in to the app.
2. Delivery personnel navigates to the delivery section.
3. Delivery personnel selects a user profile for misconduct.
4. Delivery personnel clicks the report user button.
5. Delivery personnel confirms the report.
Expected Result: User is reported successfully, and a notification is sent to the relevant authorities (FDO).

Title: User Receives Notification for an Action
Preconditions: User is logged in and an action involving the user is performed
Test Steps:
1. An action involving the user is performed (e.g., a report, a message, or a delivery update).
2. User remains logged in.
Expected Result: User receives a notification about the action in the app.

Title: Confirmation of Notification Viewing
Preconditions: User has an unread notification in the app
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the notification in the app interface.
2. User confirms having viewed the notification by clicking an appropriate button or action.
Expected Result: The system records that the user has read the notification.

Title: Direct Message Button Functionality
Preconditions: User is not under communication revocation
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the chat bubble-shaped button in the app.
2. User initiates a message to another user (client).
Expected Result: The messaging interface opens, allowing the user to communicate with the client for pickup coordination.

Title: Access User Profile Settings
Preconditions: User is registered in the app
Test Steps:
1. User taps the profile icon in the app.
2. User navigates to the profile settings section.
Expected Result: User can view personal data, change password, and submit reports or signals.

Title: Password Change in Profile
Preconditions: User is logged in and has access to profile settings
Test Data: Current password, new password (meeting complexity requirements)
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the profile settings.
2. User selects the option to change password.
3. User enters current password, new password, and confirms.
Expected Result: Password is updated successfully, and user is notified of the change.

Title: Create New Product Post
Preconditions: User is registered
Test Data: Product details (title, description), valid image file
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the designated icon to create a new post.
2. User fills in the product details in the form.
3. User clicks the camera button to upload an image.
4. User confirms submission.
Expected Result: Product post is created and displayed in the user’s profile or marketplace feed.

Title: User Availability Notification for Unavailability
Preconditions: User is marked as unavailable for a pickup
Test Steps:
1. System detects user unavailability for a scheduled pickup.
2. System sends a notification to the user.
Expected Result: User receives an alert and an associated warning for unavailability.

Title: User Reporting for Misconduct
Preconditions: User observes inappropriate behavior from another user
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the reporting section.
2. User selects the option to report misconduct.
3. User submits details of the inappropriate behavior.
Expected Result: Report is logged, and the reported user receives an internal warning or is subject to deletion based on repeat offenses.

Title: Permanent Deletion for Fraud or Repeated Violations
Preconditions: User has committed fraud or received multiple warnings
Test Steps:
1. System detects fraudulent activity or repeated violations by the user.
2. System triggers the deletion process.
Expected Result: User account is permanently deleted from Firebase, and all associated data is removed.

Title: User Registration
Preconditions: None
Test Steps:
1. User opens the app and selects the registration option.
2. User enters required personal information.
3. User confirms registration.
Expected Result: User account is created in Firebase, and user is logged in successfully.

Title: User Bug Reporting
Preconditions: User encounters a bug or issue in the app
Test Data: Description of the bug, steps to reproduce
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the bug reporting section.
2. User fills in the bug details and reproduction steps.
3. User submits the report.
Expected Result: Bug report is logged in the system for review by developers.

Title: User Action Confirmation Display
Preconditions: User is logged in and performs an action (e.g., purchase, message send)
Test Steps:
1. User initiates an action requiring confirmation (e.g., sends a direct message).
2. System displays a confirmation dialog/popup.
3. User confirms or cancels the action.
Expected Result: Action is executed or canceled based on user choice, with appropriate feedback (e.g., success/error message).

Title: Direct Messaging Functionality
Preconditions: User is logged in and another user is available for messaging
Test Data: Valid recipient username, sample message text
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the direct messaging feature.
2. User selects a recipient from the list.
3. User composes and sends a message.
Expected Result: Message is delivered to the recipient’s inbox, and sender receives a confirmation (e.g., "Message sent").

Title: Automatic Data Removal on Uninstall
Preconditions: Application is installed and user data exists (e.g., messages, profiles)
Test Steps: 1. User uninstalls the application from the device.
Expected Result: All application data (local storage, cached files, user profiles) is permanently deleted from the device.

Title: Data Encryption and Isolation
Preconditions: User data is stored in the application
Test Data: Sensitive user data (e.g., purchase history, personal details)
Test Steps:
1. User performs an action storing sensitive data (e.g., saves payment details).
2. System encrypts the data before storage.
3. Attempt to access data via external tools/applications.
Expected Result: Data remains encrypted and inaccessible to unauthorized applications or tools.

Title: Android Version Compatibility
Preconditions: Device runs Android 7.0 or higher
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on a device with Android 7.0, 8.0, 9.0, and 10+.
2. Perform basic actions (e.g., login, send message).
Expected Result: Application installs and functions without crashes or compatibility errors on all tested Android versions.

Title: Response Time and Resource Usage
Preconditions: Device has stable internet (5 Mb/s+) and no background apps running
Test Steps:
1. User performs rapid actions (e.g., sends 10 messages in 30 seconds).
2. Monitor CPU, memory, and battery usage via device tools.
3. Measure time between action initiation and completion (e.g., message send).
Expected Result: No lag or freezing; response time ≤ 2 seconds per action; resource usage remains below 30% CPU and 50% RAM.

Title: Application Size Limit
Preconditions: Application is built and ready for distribution
Test Steps: 1. Check the installed size of the application on a test device.
Expected Result: Application size is ≤ 100 MB.

Title: Mandatory Internet Connectivity
Preconditions: Device has no active internet connection
Test Steps: 1. User attempts to use core features (e.g., send message, view marketplace).
Expected Result: Application blocks actions requiring internet; displays error message: "Internet connection required (min 5 Mb/s)."

Title: Secure Data Transmission
Preconditions: User is sending/receiving sensitive data (e.g., payment info)
Test Steps:
1. User initiates a data-sensitive action (e.g., completes a purchase).
2. Monitor network traffic using packet analyzer tools.
Expected Result: Data is transmitted via HTTPS/TLS; no plaintext data is intercepted during transmission.

Title: First-time user authentication
Preconditions: User installs the app correctly
Test Steps:
1. User opens the app for the first time.
2. User taps on the login/register option.
3. User enters a valid email and password.
4. User confirms registration.
Expected Result: User is successfully authenticated and can access the app.

Title: Password modification by authenticated user
Preconditions: User is already authenticated
Test Data: Current password, new password, confirm new password
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the app.
2. User navigates to the profile or settings section.
3. User selects the option to change password.
4. User enters current password, new password, and confirms the new password.
5. User submits the changes.
Expected Result: Password is updated successfully, and user can log in with the new password.

Title: User reports another user
Preconditions: User has interacted with the target user (e.g., completed a transaction or chat)
Test Data: Target user ID, reason for reporting (e.g., inappropriate behavior)
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the app.
2. User navigates to the target user’s profile or interaction history.
3. User selects the option to report the user.
4. User enters the reason for reporting.
5. User confirms the report submission.
Expected Result: Report is submitted successfully, and the target user is flagged for review.

Title: User reports a bug
Preconditions: User is authenticated
Test Data: Bug description, steps to reproduce, screenshots (if applicable)
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the app.
2. User navigates to the bug reporting section.
3. User enters a detailed description of the bug.
4. User provides steps to reproduce the issue.
5. User attaches screenshots if needed.
6. User submits the bug report.
Expected Result: Bug report is submitted successfully, and a confirmation message appears.

Title: App sends notifications to authenticated user
Preconditions: User is authenticated
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the app.
2. App triggers a notification (e.g., system update, message, or alert).
3. User navigates to the notifications section.
Expected Result: Notification appears in the notifications section with relevant details.

Title: Buyer sends message to seller about product interest
Preconditions: User is authenticated, at least one product listing exists
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the app.
2. User views a product listing.
3. User selects the option to contact the seller.
4. User enters a message expressing interest in the product.
5. User submits the message.
Expected Result: Chat is initiated between buyer and seller, and the message is delivered successfully.

Title: App installation on minimum supported Android device
Preconditions: None
Test Data: Android 7.0 device with 100 MB free storage and 512 MB RAM
Test Steps:
1. User downloads the app from the store.
2. User installs the app on an Android 7.0 device with 100 MB free storage and 512 MB RAM.
3. User opens the app.
Expected Result: App installs and launches without errors.

Title: App functionality with minimum internet speed
Preconditions: App is installed and opened
Test Steps:
1. User connects to a network with 5 Mb/s speed.
2. User attempts to perform an action requiring internet (e.g., login, load product listings).
Expected Result: App functions as expected without performance degradation or errors.
